section: JEFP109 – The Book That Saved the Earth
title: The Book That Saved the Earth – Flashcard Concept Mastery
author: Claire Boiko

questions.question: Why is the twentieth century called the ‘Era of the Book’?
questions.correct_answer: Because books were the main source of knowledge and learning
questions.explanation: People depended on books for education, information, and guidance in daily life.

questions.question: Who is the Great and Mighty Think-Tank?
questions.correct_answer: The ruler and commander-in-chief of Mars
questions.explanation: Think-Tank is the Martian leader who plans to invade Earth.

questions.question: Where do the Martians land on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Martians mistake the library for a strange Earth structure.

questions.question: What do the Martians think books are?
questions.explanation: Due to ignorance, the Martians misunderstand books completely.

questions.question: Why does Think-Tank believe Earth is dangerous?
questions.correct_answer: He misinterprets nursery rhymes as threats
questions.explanation: Think-Tank takes the rhymes literally and assumes Earth has powerful weapons.

questions.question: What role do nursery rhymes play in the play?
questions.correct_answer: They confuse the Martians and stop the invasion
questions.explanation: The Martians’ literal interpretation of rhymes creates fear.

questions.question: How does Earth escape the Martian invasion?
questions.correct_answer: Through the Martians’ misunderstanding of books
questions.explanation: Books frighten the Martians, leading them to cancel the invasion.

questions.question: What quality of Think-Tank leads to failure?
questions.explanation: Think-Tank never accepts correction and jumps to wrong conclusions.

questions.question: Why is the title 'The Book That Saved the Earth' ironic?
questions.correct_answer: A simple book prevents a powerful invasion
questions.explanation: An ordinary book becomes a weapon stronger than arms.

questions.question: What message does the play convey?
questions.correct_answer: Knowledge and books are powerful tools
questions.explanation: The play highlights the importance of reading and understanding.

notes.title: Power of Books
notes.content: Books act as silent protectors by spreading knowledge and wisdom.

notes.title: Humour and Satire
notes.content: The play uses humour to criticise ignorance and arrogance.

facts.fact: The Martians flee Earth without any battle.
Mother Goose rhymes confuse the Martians the most.
